positioning: Track.Lab workout library for deterministic calculators. No AI coach, no database dependency, no fake/generated athlete data, no automatic prescription.
safetyPrinciples: All workouts are static templates, not personalized prescriptions.
User must manually enter pace, duration, HR zone, rest duration, race target, or result before formulas calculate output.
High-intensity templates include recovery and injury-pain safety flags.
Long-duration templates include fueling and hydration hooks where relevant.
Track.Lab should calculate and validate; it must not auto-coach or hallucinate readiness.

workouts.name: 15 min Recovery Run — Beginner road
workouts.purpose: Promote recovery with very low mechanical and metabolic stress.
workouts.bestUsedFor: Day after hard workout, return-to-running day, low-energy day, or low-intensity volume filler.
workouts.notIdealFor: When user needs a quality stimulus, feels sharp pain, or has fatigue that changes running form.
workouts.warmup: 3-8 min very easy jog or brisk walk
workouts.cooldown: 3-8 min walk or very easy jog
workouts.limitation: Static recovery template; output depends on user-entered pace or HR zone. Variant note: Recovery intent only if effort remains very easy.

workouts.name: 25 min Easy Run — Beginner road
workouts.purpose: Build aerobic capacity and repeatable low-intensity running consistency.
workouts.bestUsedFor: Routine easy day, base phase, non-quality day, or weekly mileage support.
workouts.notIdealFor: As replacement for race-specific intervals, or when user needs full rest.
workouts.limitation: Easy pace must be manually entered or derived from transparent formula input. Variant note: If HR/RPE drifts upward, user should slow down or shorten.

workouts.name: 60 min Long Run — Beginner Intermediate road
workouts.purpose: Build endurance, aerobic durability, and confidence over longer continuous running.
workouts.bestUsedFor: Weekly endurance day, base phase, 10K-HM-marathon preparation.
workouts.notIdealFor: Immediately after hard intervals, when pain changes gait, or during severe fatigue.
workouts.recovery: None; optional short planned walk for fluids
workouts.limitation: Distance depends on user pace; Track.Lab calculates estimates and must not force prescriptions. Variant note: Long-run suitability depends on weekly mileage and recovery state.

workouts.name: 5 x 30s Hill Repeats — 3-5%
workouts.purpose: Build hill strength, aerobic power, and form under uphill load.
workouts.bestUsedFor: Strength endurance block, hilly race preparation, cross-country preparation.
workouts.notIdealFor: Injury flare-up, steep technical descent, or race week overload.
workouts.mainSet: 5 x 30 sec uphill at hard controlled effort
workouts.recovery: Jog/walk down or 90 sec-3 min easy
workouts.limitation: Flat pace is unreliable uphill; effort/HR/RPE should be prioritized. Variant note: Use effort rather than flat-ground pace.
